Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Guido van Rossum Interviews Thomas Wouters (Python Core Dev) 60 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. The Xkcd thing, now interactive 93 upvotes, 10 comments
  3. C64: Putting Sprite Multiplexing to Work 18 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. First in-utero stem cell therapy for fetal spina bifida repair is safe: study 315 upvotes, 58 comments
  5. Physicists developing a quantum computer that’s entirely open source 134 upvotes, 24 comments
  6. The Cathode Ray Tube site 60 upvotes, 11 comments
  7. Show HN: I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch 451 upvotes, 131 comments
  8. Simple screw counter 169 upvotes, 43 comments
  9. Inside the M4 Apple Neural Engine, Part 1: Reverse Engineering 352 upvotes, 103 comments
  10. Seed of Might Color Correction Process (2023) [pdf] 92 upvotes, 23 comments
  11. Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS 2223 upvotes, 813 comments
  12. DOS Memory Management 64 upvotes, 17 comments
  13. How to sew a Hyperbolic Blanket (2021) 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Privacy-preserving age and identity verification via anonymous credentials 38 upvotes, 9 comments
  15. I built a pint-sized Macintosh 46 upvotes, 12 comments
  16. Launch HN: OctaPulse (YC W26) – Robotics and computer vision for fish farming 102 upvotes, 35 comments
  17. Show HN: Govbase – Follow a bill from source text to news bias to social posts 197 upvotes, 80 comments
  18. British Columbia is permanently adopting daylight time 886 upvotes, 437 comments
  19. Arm's Cortex X925: Reaching Desktop Performance 129 upvotes, 56 comments
  20. Meta’s AI smart glasses and data privacy concerns 1171 upvotes, 669 comments
  21. We Built a Video Rendering Engine by Lying to the Browser About What Time It Is 65 upvotes, 29 comments
  22. Ars Technica fires reporter after AI controversy involving fabricated quotes 344 upvotes, 209 comments
  23. Buckle Up for Bumpier Skies 49 upvotes, 25 comments
  24. China's 450kmph bullet train is the fastest ever built 22 upvotes, 12 comments
  25. Guilty Displeasures 89 upvotes, 87 comments
  26. Ask HN: Who is hiring? (March 2026) 220 upvotes, 265 comments
  27. Mullvad VPN: Banned TV Ad in the Streets of London [video] 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. The beauty and terror of modding Windows 28 upvotes, 28 comments
  29. iPhone 17e 296 upvotes, 430 comments
  30. New iPad Air, powered by M4 412 upvotes, 643 comments

Last generated 8:30pm, 3 March 2026. Source here.
